elements.text: The sky over the Ashfall Wastes bruises to a deep, toxic violet at dusk. Ash drifts down like gray snow, dusting Kaelen’s shoulders as he sits cross-legged on a jagged outcrop of black basalt. Before him lies a chipped iron blade and three clouded spirit-shards.
The standard manuals suggest a linear progression. Inhale, circulate, crystallize. But the ambient density of these depths is too low for standard equations.

He closes his eyes and draws in a breath of the sulfurous air. The crude spiritual energy of the waste resists him instantly—jagged, chaotic, tearing at his unrefined meridians. He grimaces as a thin line of blood wells at the corner of his lip.
Friction is the variable. The energy isn't resisting; it's colliding. I must adjust the internal vectors to match the jagged pressure of the basin.

He realigns his breathing. Instead of forcing a smooth flow, he segments the intake, fracturing his Qi into tiny, high-frequency pulses that mirror the harsh atmospheric vibration. The chaotic energy suddenly clicks, rushing down his spine like liquid mercury.

A faint, metallic hum resonates from his chest. The chipped blade on his lap shivers. A tiny spark of silver light ignites within his palm, absorbing a faint layer of gray ash before settling into his skin. He opens his eyes, his gaze sharper, clearer.
An efficiency increase of twelve percent. A functional baseline, but insufficient for long-term survival in this sector.

Kaelen stands, his gaze drifting past the barren ravine. Down the ridge, the flickering, crude torches of a fortified encampment cut through the gathering gloom—the territory of the local strongman. His eyes narrow, tracking the distant movements with cold, predatory precision.
He controls the superior marrow-stones. To remain in this alcove is a mathematical dead end. His perimeter is the next logical acquisition.
